raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 1918 will create the California Reproductive Health Service Corps to recruit, train, and retain a diverse workforce of health care professionals.",
    "The establishment of a Reproductive Health Service Corps is crucial following reports of a potential Supreme Court decision that may overturn Roe v. Wade.",
    "The California Reproductive Health Service Corps aims to address the lack of abortion providers in California, where 40% of counties do not have one.",
    "AB 1918 will provide educational scholarships, living wage stipends, and loan repayment options to support health care providers.",
    "The Future of Abortion Council reported that California could see an increase in out-of-state patients seeking abortion care from 46,000 to 1.4 million.",
    "AB 1918 proposes to improve training for abortion services to ensure timely care for Californians and those traveling from out of state.",
    "There is a significant gap in training opportunities for healthcare providers in reproductive health services, particularly for advanced practice clinicians and RNs.",
    "The disparities in access to healthcare and training significantly affect providers of color and communities of color."
  ]
}
```
